_comment: Gold doc IDs match the DOC_ID headers in data/hero_case/*. single_hop = answerable from ONE doc (vector should do fine). multi_hop = requires connecting >=2 docs, usually across jurisdictions (where graph should win). Noise-corpus queries use file stems (e.g. 'incident_042') as gold IDs.

queries.query: What tool caused the marks on the rear door at 14 Maple Heights Drive?
What vehicle did a witness see near the Riverside Lane burglary?
When did the 2025 Maple Heights Court burglary happen and what was taken?
How did the offender get into 902 Riverside Lane?
What partial license plate was reported in the 2023 Maple Heights case?
Who was arrested for the 2025 Maple Heights Court burglary?
Is there forensic evidence linking the Maple Heights and Riverside burglaries?
Across all three burglaries, what is consistent about how the offender broke in?
Which cases share both the same 8mm tool signature and a dark blue sedan?
Was a regional or cross-jurisdiction database check ever recommended, and what happened to that recommendation?
Do the Maple Heights 2023 and 2025 cases involve the same tool?
What evidence ties Daniel Marsh to the Riverside burglary specifically?
If you only had the two 2023 cases, what shared signals would suggest a single serial offender?
Why wasn't the serial pattern caught earlier across the two departments?
Connect the dark blue sedan sightings across every case that mentions one.
Which forensic reports describe a blade with a nick on the left edge?
Does Daniel Marsh's alibi for the night of the Riverside burglary hold up against the other evidence?
Is there any record that places the suspect near the Riverside scene on the night he claims he was out of state?
Which Oak Park incident reports describe a burglary committed using a bump key on the front door?
Find incident reports where the vehicle of interest was a red Ford F-150 pickup with rusted wheel wells.
Which Westfield residential burglary reports mention a partial shoe impression photographed on the porch?
Which incident reports describe a motor vehicle theft using a key-fob relay device with no physical entry marks?
Are there multiple Lakeview residential burglaries with the same forced-basement-window method that could indicate a pattern?
Which commercial burglary reports across different jurisdictions mention the offender cutting power before entry, and do any involve the same suspect description?
Across all Glenwood and Cedar Falls residential reports, are there recurring MO elements that suggest the same offender operated in both cities?
Connect all car theft reports that describe a vehicle being recovered stripped in an adjacent jurisdiction — do they share any suspect vehicle descriptions?
